step2_rag_chunks: If someone offers you a cigarette, practice politely saying no and reminding yourself why you decided to quit.

If you have tried to quit before, that experience is actually useful information â€” not a failure. What worked even a little? What made it harder? Most people who successfully quit have tried several times before it sticks. Each attempt teaches you something about your own pattern.
Nicotine addiction is both physical and psychological. Addressing both aspects can improve success in quitting.

step2_rag_chunks: Stress is a common trigger for smoking. Relaxation techniques such as deep breathing, meditation, or short walks can help manage stress without using tobacco.
Nicotine addiction is both physical and psychological. Addressing both aspects can improve success in quitting.
Keeping your hands busy with activities such as drawing, writing, or using a stress ball can help reduce the urge to smoke.

step2_rag_chunks: Avoid keeping cigarettes, lighters, or ashtrays nearby when trying to quit smoking.
Nicotine replacement therapy such as nicotine gum, patches, or lozenges can help reduce withdrawal symptoms and cravings.

One sentence only. Nothing more.
